name: OSK restored retinal epigenetic state and vision in mice
summary: Lu 2020 supports tissue-specific functional plausibility: OSK expression in mouse retinal ganglion cells restored youthful DNA methylation patterns and improved axon regeneration and vision outcomes in injury, glaucoma, and aging models.
statement: AAV-delivered OSK in mouse retinal ganglion cells restored youthful DNA methylation and transcriptomic patterns, promoted axon regeneration after injury, and reversed vision loss in glaucoma and aged-mouse models.
population_or_model: Mouse retinal ganglion cells in CNS injury, glaucoma, and aged-mouse vision models
time_horizon: Post-AAV OSK induction in tissue-specific mouse experiments
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract states functional reversal of vision loss but does not report a single effect size.
caveats: This is tissue-specific animal evidence in ocular/CNS models.
The result does not establish systemic aging benefit or human efficacy.
The abstract reports conflicts and patents connected to epigenetic reprogramming therapies.
